determineWinner.name: Determine the winner
determineWinner.description: Get both players' choices and announce the correct result: "Yuki", "Ando", or "tie".

paperVsPaper.name: Paper vs Paper
paperVsPaper.description: It's a draw. Announce it correctly!

paperVsRock.name: Paper vs Rock
paperVsRock.description: Yuki's paper beats Ando's rock. Announce Yuki as the winner!

paperVsScissors.name: Paper vs Scissors
paperVsScissors.description: Ando's scissors beat Yuki's paper. Announce Ando as the winner.

rockVsPaper.name: Rock vs Paper
rockVsPaper.description: Ando's paper beats Yuki's rock. Announce Ando as the winner.

rockVsRock.name: Rock vs Rock
rockVsRock.description: It's a draw. Announce it correctly!

rockVsScissors.name: Rock vs Scissors
rockVsScissors.description: Yuki's rock beats Ando's scissors. Announce Yuki as the winner.

scissorsVsPaper.name: Scissors vs Paper
scissorsVsPaper.description: Yuki's scissors beat Ando's paper. Announce Yuki as the winner.

scissorsVsRock.name: Scissors vs Rock
scissorsVsRock.description: Ando's rock beats Yuki's scissors. Announce Ando as the winner.

scissorsVsScissors.name: Scissors vs Scissors
scissorsVsScissors.description: It's a draw. Announce it correctly!

howToStart.question: How do I start?
howToStart.answer: Get Yuki's and Ando's choices using the provided functions and store them in variables so you can compare them.

simplestCase.question: What's the simplest case to handle first?
simplestCase.answer: A tie. If both choices are equal, you can announce a tie straight away and skip the rest.

checkCombination.question: How do I check a specific winning combination?
checkCombination.answer: Use `&&` (and) to combine two conditions that must both be true, e.g. "Yuki chose rock AND Ando chose scissors" means Yuki wins.

shorterWay.question: I'm ending up with loads of `if` statements. Is there a shorter way?
shorterWay.answer: Default the result to one player winning, then only check the three combinations where the other player wins. That cuts the cases roughly in half.

invalidChoice.question: What if a player enters something other than rock, paper or scissors?
invalidChoice.answer: You don't need to worry about that here. The provided functions only ever return one of the three valid choices.

getYukiChoice.description: Returns Yuki's choice — one of `"rock"`, `"paper"`, or `"scissors"`.

getAndoChoice.description: Returns Ando's choice — one of `"rock"`, `"paper"`, or `"scissors"`.

announceResult.description: Announces the result of the game. Pass `"Yuki"`, `"Ando"`, or `"tie"`.
